title: NPU virtual environment strategy: prefer --system-site-packages over external mirror pip install
problem_description: Initial venv creation used `pip install -r requirements.txt` from Aliyun mirror, which fetched CUDA-native packages (torch 2.11.0 with nvidia-cublas, nvidia-nccl, etc.) incompatible with the Ascend NPU host. The system Python already had torch 2.5.1+cpu and torch-npu 2.5.1 pre-installed. Recreating the venv with --system-site-packages resolved the issue by inheriting the correct NPU-compatible packages.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Before creating a new venv on an NPU host, check if system Python already has torch and torch-npu installed via `python3 -c "import torch_npu; print(torch_npu.__version__)"`. 2. If NPU packages are pre-installed, create venv with `python3 -m venv --system-site-packages .venv` to inherit them. 3. Only install additional non-torch project dependencies via pip install. 4. Verify with `python -c "import torch; import torch_npu; print(torch.__version__)"` inside the venv.
artifact_evidence: execution_journal.jsonl (timing records of venv creation and reinstall)
reasoning: NPU hosts in production and cloud environments typically come pre-configured with NPU-compatible PyTorch stacks. Fetching from standard PyPI mirrors (including domestic ones like Aliyun) pulls x86/CUDA variants that are incompatible. This experience generalizes to all Ascend NPU environments with pre-installed toolchains and prevents wasted time installing and discarding wrong packages.
